title: Nunnezharia regia
intro: Nunnezharia regia, also known as Wild Yam, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its tuberous root system and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including alleviating menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and nausea, due to its estrogen-like properties. The bioactive constituents of Nunnezharia regia include diosgenin, a steroid saponin, as well as other steroidal saponins, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Nunnezharia regia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, capsules, and dried root infusions, often marketed as a natural remedy for hormonal imbalances and menopausal symptoms.
